strengths: 15+ years of hands-on software engineering across cloud, AI, and infrastructure domains, with direct experience in AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq.
Proven entrepreneurial initiative through founding and sustaining GISCollective — an open-source AI-adjacent geospatial platform used by NGOs — demonstrating product vision and full-stack ownership.
Deep technical fluency in AI-relevant tooling (DLang, Node.js, Kubernetes, AWS CDK) and a track record of building high-performance, scalable systems under real-world constraints.
concerns: No explicit evidence of a Master’s or PhD in an AI-related field, though practical experience may compensate.
Limited documentation of direct AI/ML model development or published AI products — contributions are infrastructure and tooling focused.
German language proficiency is listed as basic, which may pose minor communication challenges in a Berlin-based, customer-facing role.
response.justification: James Smith brings exactly the 15+ years of engineering depth required, with demonstrable, recent work in AI-agent infrastructure and cloud tooling at re:cinq, alongside a decade-long track record of building scalable, real-world platforms — including an open-source geospatial system used by NGOs — that mirror the strategic, builder-oriented mindset the role demands. His entrepreneurial initiative, technical versatility across cloud, AI-adjacent systems, and infrastructure, and consistent pattern of shipping complex, user-centric tools make him a compelling fit despite lacking a formal AI advanced degree.
